King of the Hill Tournament
Administration Software

For a school project we were given the simple instructions to create piece software in Java that utilized a Stack or Queue. As I've been into competitive gaming for a while, I decided to do a tournament organizer. I chose the King of the Hill format because it was a prime example of a queue, as people wait in line for their chance to compete for the spot of the king. Though this isn't the most complex program I've written, It's one of the ones I'm most proud of.

Current Feature List

I'm particularly proud of the way that I modularized the program, which was done in a way such that the user interface is interchangeable. For the purpose of the project, I created a console-based UI, but one could easily create a GUI and use that instead. The modularization also makes the code highly maintainable, so it will be easier to fix bugs and add new features.

The source code, including JUnit tests, are available as a .zip here.
